items.title: Japan’s Katayama: Fluctuating oil prices cause high volatility in financial and FX markets
items.summary: Japan’s Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said on Tuesday that G7 finance ministers and central bankers agreed that fluctuating oil prices cause high volatility in financial and foreign exchange markets.

items.title: US President Donald Trump: Iran ceasefire proposal ‘significant’ but ‘not good enough’
items.summary: US President Donald Trump said that the latest proposal for a US ceasefire with Iran is “not good enough,” ahead of his deadline for Iran to either reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face major attacks on its civilian infrastructure, CNBC reported on Monday.

items.title: NZD/USD rises as ceasefire hopes weigh on US Dollar ahead of RBNZ decision
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair trades with a bullish tone near the 0.5710 region on Tuesday, as the US Dollar (USD) softens amid improving risk sentiment driven by ceasefire hopes in the Middle East.

items.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average rises as ceasefire talks lift sentiment
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) gained a scant 120 points, or 0.3%, on Monday in the first session following the Good Friday market closure. The index pushed toward 46,700 in early trading before fading through the midday session and ultimately settling around 46,500.

items.title: Forex Today: US Dollar slips on Iran ceasefire hopes as Oil stays elevated and Gold steadies
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) fell towards the 100.00 area on Monday as markets were weighed by United States (US) President Donald Trump’s latest Strait of Hormuz ultimatum against growing hopes for a ceasefire framework between the US and Iran.

items.title: Vietnam: Inflation surge and SBV policy stance – UOB
items.summary: UOB Global Economics & Markets Research highlights that Vietnam’s headline CPI jumped to 4.65% year-on-year in March 2026, driven by higher energy costs, pushing inflation above the State Bank of Vietnam’s 4.5% target.

items.title: WTI Crude Oil markets see fresh volatility as Trump deadline looms
items.summary: WTI Crude Oil saw sharply diverging price action across the spot and futures markets on Monday. May futures spiked to about $115 in early dealing, before pulling back near $112, roughly flat against Thursday's settlement.

items.title: US Dollar Index recovers to 100 as Iran rejects ceasefire, inflation-heavy week looms
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) struggled to crimp downside momentum near the 100.00 handle on Monday after a volatile day that saw the index slide from overnight highs near 100.30 down to a session low near 99.75 before staging a late recovery.

items.title: APAC FX: Intervention and vulnerability to USD – BNY
items.summary: BNY’s Bob Savage focuses on rising FX intervention and fragile sentiment in Asia-Pacific (APAC) as higher Oil and geopolitical risks pressure regional currencies.

items.title: CAD: Jobs data and geopolitics steer rates – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities strategists Robert Both and Emma Lawrence highlight that Canadian rates are opening weaker, with yields tracking US moves and geopolitical tensions. They expect CAD employment to show only a modest rebound and see imported volatility dominating.

items.title: Brent: Conflict keeps risk premium elevated – OCBC
items.summary: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong argue that market hopes for de-escalation in the US–Iran conflict look premature for Brent.

items.title: When is the US ISM Services PMI data for March and how could it affect EUR/USD?
items.summary: The United States (US) Institute of Supply Management (ISM) Services Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) data for March is scheduled to be published today at 14:00 GMT.
